evals.prompt: tạo project mới tên là Bookings, dùng PostgreSQL, output directory là /tmp/scaffold-test/bookings
evals.expected_output: Complete Clean Architecture solution with 4 source projects (Domain, Application, Infrastructure, API), 5 test projects, Directory.Build.props, Directory.Packages.props, .slnx file, and all base abstractions. dotnet build and dotnet test should pass.
expectations: build_result.txt shows 'Build succeeded' with 0 errors
test_result.txt shows ArchitectureTests project passed with 0 failed tests
tree.txt contains a .slnx file at the solution root (Bookings.slnx), not a .sln file
All 5 test projects exist in tree.txt: Bookings.Domain.UnitTests, Bookings.Application.UnitTests, Bookings.Infrastructure.IntegrationTests, Bookings.API.IntegrationTests, Bookings.ArchitectureTests
Directory.Packages.props contains ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=true
No PackageReference entry in any .csproj file has a Version attribute (CPM correctly configured)
Infrastructure DependencyInjection.cs uses UseNpgsql to configure the database (not UseSqlServer)
BaseEntity.cs, IRepository.cs, IUnitOfWork.cs, Result.cs, and Error.cs all exist in the Domain layer
ValidationBehavior.cs exists in the Application/Behaviors directory
Generated .cs files use file-scoped namespace syntax (e.g. 'namespace Bookings.Domain;' not 'namespace Bookings.Domain { }')

evals.prompt: Create a new Clean Architecture project called EcommerceApi using SQL Server. Put it in /tmp/scaffold-test/ecommerce
evals.expected_output: Complete Clean Architecture solution using SQL Server (Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.SqlServer instead of Npgsql). All 4 source layers and 5 test projects created. Should use SqlServer in DbContext configuration. Build must pass.
expectations: build_result.txt shows 'Build succeeded' with 0 errors
test_result.txt shows ArchitectureTests project passed with 0 failed tests
tree.txt contains a .slnx file at the solution root (EcommerceApi.slnx), not a .sln file

All 5 test projects exist in tree.txt: EcommerceApi.Domain.UnitTests, EcommerceApi.Application.UnitTests, EcommerceApi.Infrastructure.IntegrationTests, EcommerceApi.API.IntegrationTests, EcommerceApi.ArchitectureTests
Directory.Packages.props contains ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=true
No PackageReference entry in any .csproj file has a Version attribute (CPM correctly configured)
Infrastructure DependencyInjection.cs uses UseSqlServer to configure the database (not UseNpgsql)
BaseEntity.cs, IRepository.cs, IUnitOfWork.cs, Result.cs, and Error.cs all exist in the Domain layer
ValidationBehavior.cs exists in the Application/Behaviors directory
Generated .cs files use file-scoped namespace syntax (e.g. 'namespace EcommerceApi.Domain;' not 'namespace EcommerceApi.Domain { }')

evals.prompt: tạo solution TaskManager ở /tmp/scaffold-test/taskmgr, dùng PostgreSQL
evals.expected_output: Complete Clean Architecture solution named TaskManager. All structure correct. .slnx file present. All abstractions (BaseEntity, IRepository, IUnitOfWork, Result, Error, ICommand, IQuery, etc.) created. Architecture tests pass.
expectations: build_result.txt shows 'Build succeeded' with 0 errors
test_result.txt shows ArchitectureTests project passed with 0 failed tests
tree.txt contains a .slnx file at the solution root (TaskManager.slnx), not a .sln file

All 5 test projects exist in tree.txt: TaskManager.Domain.UnitTests, TaskManager.Application.UnitTests, TaskManager.Infrastructure.IntegrationTests, TaskManager.API.IntegrationTests, TaskManager.ArchitectureTests
Directory.Packages.props contains ManagePackageVersionsCentrally=true
No PackageReference entry in any .csproj file has a Version attribute (CPM correctly configured)
Infrastructure DependencyInjection.cs uses UseNpgsql to configure the database (not UseSqlServer)
BaseEntity.cs, IRepository.cs, IUnitOfWork.cs, Result.cs, and Error.cs all exist in the Domain layer
ValidationBehavior.cs exists in the Application/Behaviors directory
Generated .cs files use file-scoped namespace syntax (e.g. 'namespace TaskManager.Domain;' not 'namespace TaskManager.Domain { }')
